title: How ChatGPT changed my workflow and decreased the risk from 17% to 10%.
author: Kindly_Preference_54
selftext:
Hi everyone,

2.5 months ago ChatGPT helped me build a new backtesting routine that was much more systematic and deeper than anything I used before. I was blown away by how well it got to understand the specifics of trading. I trade quantitative strategies so the backtesting process is the core of what I do. 

This new workflow decreased my Value at Risk from 17% to 10%, exactly as GPT promised: 

1. **Optimization (3 months by Recovery Factor)**.
2. **OOS1 (9 months)**. The most important phase. Here I strictly filter setups by RF grades and recovery behavior (frequency and duration). The latter is analyzed by GPT (when I am too lazy :). Grades: >=2.0: excellent; 1.5-2.0: good; 1.2-1.5: weak; =<1.2: reject.
3. **OOS2 (full year before OOS1)**. This phase is used to understand robustness and regime sensitivity: >=1.3: robust; 1.0-1.3: regime-sensitive; =<1.0: fragile. A weak result here does not automatically reject the setup, but it signals higher risk and affects position sizing.
4. **OOS3 - Stress tests (worst risk off periods)**: the purpose here is survival only. The setup is rejected only if recovery logic breaks and drawdown goes wild.
5. **Repeat steps 1-4 every 2 months.**

comments.author: BackTesting-Queen
comments.body: Congratulations on the improvement in your Value at Risk! Your systematic approach to backtesting is commendable. The phased approach you've adopted, with optimization, out-of-sample testing, and stress tests, is a solid framework for robust strategy development. Regularly repeating these steps is a great way to ensure your strategy stays relevant and adapts to changing market conditions. Keep in mind, the key to successful trading is not just in the strategy code, but also in your mental code. Reflect on your trades, your emotions, and your decision-making process. This will help you align your behavior with your goals and let the system, not your impulses, run the show. Keep up the good work!
